1—
1.4.’
‘aiirornia
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 5,
1992
Peter D.
Lemmon
Knox,
Lemmon
& Brady
455 Capitol Mall,
Suite 210
Sacramento,
CA
95814
Re:
Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No.
A-92--l37
Dear Mr.
Lemmon:
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to Thomas Knox of your firm on behalf of the members of the
California—Nevada Super Speed Ground Transportation Commission
(the
“SST Commission”)
under the conflict of interest disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act.
1
!
The draft letter you provided for my signature correctly
summarizes the advice I provided to Mr.
Knox.
That is:
1.
The California Commissioners of the SST Commission will
not be required to file leaving office statements of economic
interests
(Form 730)
unless and until the pending legislation
extending the SST Commission is defeated or vetoed.
It is my
understanding that the members of the Commission have continued to
engage in Commission activities since its expiration on
December 31,
1991,
and that you expect the legislation to be
signed in the near future.
2.
The SST Commissioners must file annual statements of
economic interests
(Form 730)
no later than April 1,
1992.
3.
The SST Commissioners who are members of the State
Legislature are not required to file statements of economic
interests pursuant to the Commission’s conflict of interest code.
They are required to file Form 721 pursuant to Section 87200.
Therefore, they are not required to file Form 730 on April 1,
1992,
and they are not required to file a leaving office Form 730
in connection with their positions as
SST Commissioners.
1/
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
q.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 •
(916)322-5660


Peter
D.
Lemmon
Our File No.
A-92-137
Page
2
I hope this information has adequately addressed your
concerns.
Please contact me at
(916)
322-5662 if you have
additional questions.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
By:
Carla Wardlow
Acting Division Chief
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

sections.conclusion: 1. The California Commissioners of the SST Commission will not be required to file leaving office statements of economic interests (Form 730) unless and until the pending legislation extending the SST Commission is defeated or vetoed. 2. The SST Commissioners must file annual statements of economic interests (Form 730) no later than April 1, 1992. 3. The SST Commissioners who are members of the State Legislature are not required to file statements of economic interests pursuant to the Commission's conflict of interest code. They are required to file Form 721 pursuant to Section 87200. Therefore, they are not required to file Form 730 on April 1, 1992, and they are not required to file a leaving office Form 730 in connection with their positions as SST Commissioners.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the conflict of interest disclosure and statement of economic interests filing requirements for California commissioners of the California-Nevada Super Speed Ground Transportation Commission, particularly regarding Form 730 filing deadlines and exemptions for legislative members?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: SST Commission members must file annual Form 730 statements by April 1, 1992, but need not file leaving office statements unless the pending extension legislation is defeated. Legislative members are exempt from Form 730 requirements under the conflict of interest code and instead file Form 721 pursuant to Section 87200.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is a confirmation letter of prior telephone advice. No separate QUESTION section exists; the substance of the inquiry is embedded in the introductory paragraph and the three numbered conclusions serve as the substantive response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the conflict of interest disclosure and statement of economic interests filing requirements for California commissioners of the California-Nevada Super Speed Ground Transportation Commission, particularly regarding Form 730 filing deadlines and exemptions for legislative members?

CONCLUSION: 1. The California Commissioners of the SST Commission will not be required to file leaving office statements of economic interests (Form 730) unless and until the pending legislation extending the SST Commission is defeated or vetoed. 2. The SST Commissioners must file annual statements of economic interests (Form 730) no later than April 1, 1992. 3. The SST Commissioners who are members of the State Legislature are not required to file statements of economic interests pursuant to the Commission's conflict of interest code. They are required to file Form 721 pursuant to Section 87200. Therefore, they are not required to file Form 730 on April 1, 1992, and they are not required to file a leaving office Form 730 in connection with their positions as SST Commissioners.

Lemmon Knox, Lemmon & Brady 455 Capitol Mall, Suite 210 Sacramento, CA 95814 Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice Our File No. A-92--l37 Dear Mr. Lemmon: You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to Thomas Knox of your firm on behalf of the members of the California—Nevada Super Speed Ground Transportation Commission (the “SST Commission”) under the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act. 1 ! The draft letter you provided for my signature correctly summarizes the advice I provided to Mr. Knox. That is: 1. The California Commissioners of the SST Commission will not be required to file leaving office statements of economic interests (Form 730) unless and until the pending legislation extending the SST Commission is defeated or vetoed. It is my understanding that the members of the Commission have continued to engage in Commission activities since its expiration on December 31, 1991, and that you expect the legislation to be signed in the near future. 2. The SST Commissioners must file annual statements of economic interests (Form 730) no later than April 1, 1992. 3. The SST Commissioners who are members of the State Legislature are not required to file statements of economic interests pursuant to the Commission’s conflict of interest code. They are required to file Form 721 pursuant to Section 87200. Therefore, they are not required to file Form 730 on April 1, 1992, and they are not required to file a leaving office Form 730 in connection with their positions as SST Commissioners. 1/ Government Code Sections 81000—91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, q. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 Peter D. Lemmon Our File No. A-92-137 Page 2 I hope this information has adequately addressed your concerns. Please contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional questions. Sincerely, Scott Hallabrin Acting General Counsel By: Carla Wardlow Acting Division Chief Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
embedding.summary: FPPC Acting General Counsel confirms telephone advice regarding conflict of interest disclosure requirements for the California-Nevada Super Speed Ground Transportation Commission, clarifying Form 730 filing deadlines and exemptions for state legislators serving on the Commission.
